programme.desc: A US Navy lieutenant is murdered at the Defence Logistics Agency, while visitings the office of an admiral currently away at sea. As the quest to locate the missing artwork gets under way, the agents realise they need the help of an expert. Luckily, Tony's con-artist father has returned to town, having broken up with his fiancee, and is on hand to provide expert advice as the hunt for the killer takes them into the shady world of the black market. Starring Richard Lintern, Emilia Fox, David Caves, Kaye Wragg and Hugo Becker

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The discovery of a Roman sword with traces of blood on it is linked to an unidentified, headless corpse found near Heathrow in March 2008. It leads the team members to a lock-up belonging to a bodybuilder called Mark Rix, who died of a suspected heart attack the same year, so they start looking for connections between the original murder and Rix's death. They are helped in their investigation by anthropologist Fiona Kennedy, for whom Dan begins to develop feelings - just as he is told his wife might be discharged from the secure hospital where she has spent the past several years. Tracy-Ann Obermann and Liz White guest star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Nikki is asked to give evidence at the appeal of convicted murderer David Bennetto, and when her testimony leads to his release, she ends up locking horns with DI Rachel Klein, who put the man away six years earlier. It seems the detective is right when another young man is found dead at the scene of the original crime. Has Nikki helped to free a serial killer? Emilia Fox stars, with appearances from Tobias Menzies, Michael Socha and Aaron McCusker

programme.desc: Huw and Lenny hit the airwaves, while Alan has more problems at home. Joe's on the move, and Peggy pays another visit to the hospital

programme.desc: A case of sexual assault puts Stacey in a difficult position and threatens to divide loyalties in the camp. Starring Kate O'Malley and Con O'Neill

programme.desc: James encounters a savage pig, but finds it a much less daunting challenge than making a speech at the youth club. Rural drama, staring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The roguish antiques dealer incurs the wrath of the local golf club by declaring their prize trophy is a fake - and almost gets Lady Jane expelled into the bargain. Light-hearted drama, starring Ian McShane, Chris Jury and Phyllis Logan

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: William and Hester entertain a client from the Middle East, but soon find their guest has an unusual understanding of mixing business with pleasure. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie, with Bruce Montague (Butterflies)

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr Grace offers the staff accommodation on the premises when their difficult journeys to work lead to demands for a travel allowance, but they soon find flat-sharing is not as easy as it seemed. Vintage comedy, starring John Inman and Mollie Sugden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo is having difficulty coming to terms with being less agile and becomes jealous of local athlete Ernie Burniston when he spots him riding a bike. Meanwhile, Howard tries to deliver a birthday card to Marina without Pearl finding out. Bill Owen and Robert Fyfe star in the long-running rural comedy

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo overhears Nora Batty discussing the effect Errol Flynn's riding outfit used to have on her, so he sets out to become the man of her dreams. Bill Owen stars

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: The duo are called in to find the murderer of an eight-year-old girl, a case which dredges up disturbing memories for Dalziel, who investigated a similar incident in the same area 15 years earlier. The townsfolk are sure a missing local man is guilty of both crimes, but new evidence soon points toward a more complex solution. Feature-length detective drama, starring Warren Clarke, Colin Buchanan, Joanna David, Ronald Pickup and Kaye Wragg

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The arrest of a Turkish girl who has been working illegally leads Ucos to reopen the case of Richard Gibson, a pub landlord who died in a fire in 2009. The original investigation concluded Gibson's death was probably suicide due to his dire financial situation, but the detectives have some questions about the pub's live-in barmaid, who disappeared on the night of the blaze. Could widowed landlady Joanne, pub regular Jason, or the dead man's best friend David, who happens to have a sideline in passing off his British-produced sparkling wine as French champagne, supply them with any answers? While the case tests the team's brain power, Sasha is also keen to raise their fitness levels as she signs them up for a five-a-side football competition. Phil Davis, Niamh Cusack and Tom Georgeson guest star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Nikki and Jack travel to Scotland to examine the body of a lap dancer found in a remote forest, and the search for a suspect brings them into conflict with a couple of rogue city cops. However, when more remains are discovered, it becomes clear they are looking for a serial killer - and news of the crimes preys deeply on the mind of a young mother who is harbouring a dark secret. Emilia Fox and David Caves star, with appearances from Martin Compston (Line of Duty) and Gary Lewis (Billy Elliot)

programme.title: The Bill

programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts arrives in Willenhall, West Midlands, to see a semi-detached house. Lucy Alexander is at an overgrown plot of land in west London to find out the ambitious plans of the new owner, while Dion Dublin is in Liverpool to see a particularly colourful property

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: Footage from a police body camera helps convict a violent offender who attacked a policewoman and the owners of a wine bar use facial recognition cameras to tackle pickpocketing

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Paisley crewmates Pete and Gary attend to an elderly patient who has collapsed at a supermarket. In Glasgow, John and Suzanne need to work out why a patient has collapsed at home

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from the Isle of Anglesey in north Wales, where experts Danny Sebastian and Nick Hall advise two competing teams anxious top make maximum profit at auction. Anita also meets up with gold prospector Vincent Thurkettle, who struck gold just off the nearby coast

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al is keen to try out his new game app on the team, and Karen proves especially enthusiastic. Emma is challenged when a stabbing leads to an internal police investigation

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton travels to the Warwickshire countryside to help a couple with a £600,000 budget find a home for them and their two young daughters

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Food obsessives Lily and Milly are best friends but today they become rivals as they bring in the ingredients for chefs Anna Haugh and Ellis Barrie to serve up the best dish

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The restorers work their magic on a ride-on toy snail, a ballroom trophy that has lost its shine and a vintage bicycle given to a young girl by her father

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Lily cannot understand why more is not being done to help her mum, so when Will mentions how Honey's injury brought his family together, it gives the distressed young girl an idea. Sharon considers moving into the fitness industry, while Mick receives an unwelcome phone call

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: In the aftermath of Cameron's comeuppance, Nicky vents her frustrations on a patient - and is forced to ask herself whether she really wants to raise a killer's baby. Reyhan challenges a traumatised Hanssen to tell Sahira the truth, but can he do it knowing it will destroy her? The AAU staff are surprised to see Ange back in scrubs so soon after her ordeal

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: An oil rig worker from Tyne and Wear is rushed to the A&E department of Gilbert Bain Hospital with a suspected stroke

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: The return of Kay Mellor's lottery-based drama, this time following the workers at Woodvale Kennels, whose future looks in doubt when the owners announce the business is being sold to a large corporate chain. The only lifeline for gambling addict Keeley is the work's lottery, so when she checks the ticket with local newsagent Frank, she is ecstatic to learn they have won £500. But the team soon have reason to believe Frank may have made a mistake. Now all they need to do is find him. Starring Neil Morrissey, Katherine Rose Morley, Taj Atwal and Emily Head

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Documentary highlighting how British Jews from across the faith celebrate life's big milestones, from birth to coming of age, marriage to the end of life. A 12-year-old prepares for his bar mitzvah, a couple marry in Spain, a modern ceremony they have adapted according to their beliefs, and at Bushey Cemetery in Hertfordshire, a woman describes her role as a volunteer who does the tahara, the ritual washing of the deceased before burial

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: A look at how British Hindus mark life's big milestones. In south London, Bhumi and Chirag are marking the birth of their six-day-old son with a traditional ceremony at home, inviting the gods to write his future. Meanwhile, when 18-year-old Samahit leaves for university, his family travel with him to bless his new home at the halls of residence

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman struggles to cope after Miss Watson's death, while Bradley goes to extreme lengths to find her father's killer. Prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, starring Vera Farmiga, Freddie Highmore and Nicola Peltz

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: As Norman's obsession with Miss Watson continues, Norma tries to distract his thoughts elsewhere. Meanwhile, Bradley faces problems and needs assistance, and Romero is under pressure to make an arrest. A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, starring Vera Farmiga, Freddie Highmore and Max Thieriot

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Sally Nugent and Dan Walker present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits Burton upon Trent, Staffordshire, to see a commercial space, and in Kent meets a couple who are returning to property development after taking a few years out. Dion Dublin meets a husband and wife team at a split-level flat in London

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: After an elderly couple's home is broken into, a police officer looks at burglary patterns in the area and predicts where and when the culprits are likely to strike again. Only this time, he's waiting for them

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Special Operations Paramedics are called to the scene of a road traffic accident near Kinross where a car has flipped off the road and landed in a ditch

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Antiques experts Richard Madley and Jonathan Pratt head to West Yorkshire to help two teams search for interesting - and potentially profitable - items at a fair at Wetherby Racecourse. Presenter Charlie Ross also pays a visit to the Bowes museum in Co Durham

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruhma supports one couple from the start to the end of their pregnancy journey - but she seems unable to admit when she needs help herself. Starring Bharti Patel

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah goes property shopping in rural Devon with a couple who have a £350,000 budget and hope to find a home that has both a workshop and an art studio

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Today is a family affair as Lauren and her aunt Jayne compete, hoping to learn new vegan and fish recipes from chefs Jeremy Pang and Romy Gill. Presented by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts repair a small musical table of great sentimental value, a Land Army doll made by the owner's mother and a pair of worn-out driving gloves

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Carl from Stoke brings in a key that turns out to be not quite what it appears. Naz from Halifax presents a harmonica that she wants to sell so she and her young son can have a holiday, and Martin from London tries to get rid of a piece of furniture that holds a hidden secret

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sisters Linda Atkinson and Pauline Murphy from Merseyside arrive with their mother's pride and joy - a chair created for the investiture ceremony of the Prince of Wales in 1969. One of 4,000 made and later auctioned off to the general public, it is now in need of urgent attention. Others items in need of restoration are an air-powered toy car, an RNLI donation box and a hand-tailored bear used as a Second World War mascot. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley hosts the property-based guessing game in which four people claim to own the same house, while a celebrity panel try work out which one is telling the truth. This week, regulars Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love, Jamali Maddix are joined by Rob Rinder to find the real Michael. From the first sight of the four players the panel are instantly thrown by a familiar face, while the path to the truth is obscured by another Michael's inability to remember anything about his own life

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Struggling vlogger Liam continues his mission to become an online influencer by creating a social media-friendly travel experience. Spoof documentary, starring Liam Williams, Tim Key, Arnab Chanda and Emma Sidi

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam tries to make sense of influencers by visiting mega-vlogger Millipede, who has been announced to appear in a West End musical - despite not being able to sing. Liam also attempts to save a struggling art gallery by trying to appeal to a younger audience.

programme.title: The Surrogates
programme.desc: Maddie, Alex and Richard give themselves six months to get pregnant, but as the deadline approaches, Maddie feels low and the trio begin to doubt their future as a surrogacy team. Single man David has his embryo transferred into Faye, and the pair navigate the emotional complexities of the surrogate-parent relationship

programme.title: Football Abuse: The Ugly Side of the Beautiful Game
programme.desc: Reporter Mark Daly explores allegations of a cover-up connected to a notorious paedophile scandal that rocked the youth football circuit in Scotland in the past. He also hears accounts from former footballers who talk for the first time about the abuse they allegedly suffered as boys in the sport

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin visit properties being sold at auction in Wigan, Cumbria and Liverpool, chatting to the new owners about their renovation plans

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: How a brave mother fought back against car thieves as they tried to steal her car from her driveway - with her baby inside. Narrated by Rav Wilding

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Special Operations Paramedics Mark and Leigh are called to a car crash in Livingston, where a patient needs to be fully immobilised after complaining of neck pain. Paramedic Gary and Technician Pete attend a patient in Barrhead who has been coughing up blood and needs urgent assessment. Meanwhile, in Edinburgh, Rapid Response Paramedic John is called to a patient with back pain after a fall who is reluctant to go to hospital

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp is joined by experts Danny Sebastian and David Harper at Nottingham racecourse as two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction. Natasha also goes behind the scenes at the National Justice Museum, which is based in Nottingham

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al reluctantly leads a shared patient consultation, while Bear may be out of his depth. The connection between a radio host, a graphic novelist and a timid man needs to be determined

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: After long and demanding careers in the emergency services, a hitherto busy couple are after a change of scenery in the Norfolk countryside. Jonnie Irwin guides them on their rural property tour, showing them some homes available for their £900,000 budget. Jonnie also pays a visit to 15th-century manor house Oxburgh Hall and learns the tricks of the trade used for its 21st-century restoration

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Glaswegian friends Andrew and Stephen have very different experiences of cooking - Andrew cooks mostly for his family and is hoping to get a bit more experimental, whereas Stephen loves to throw dinner parties for friends. Rylan Clark-Neal presides as the two compete, with chefs Romy Gill and Ellis Barrie helping the pair pull out all the stops to create some amazing dishes

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea tackle the motor and body of a hand-made toy racing car, while leather expert Suzie Fletcher get to grips with a leather bowls bag in need of restoration to pass onto the fifth generation of a sports-mad family. Wood expert Will Kirk works his magic on an intricately carved box from the Far East. Its owner's mother bought it with her when she came to Europe from Singapore as a young bride

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: With her hearing looming, Stacey has a fraught final showdown with Martin, while Jean reassures her daughter she will take care of everything at home. Kathy and Kim question Bobby as he waits for his second date, fully embracing his more confident new persona. Mick learns that other survivors have come forward, hopeful that they will be able to give evidence instead of him

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: The business ideas contest moves to BBC One, with entrepreneurs Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies eager to strike more potentially lucrative deals. In this first episode, a pair of tea entrepreneurs find themselves in hot water when their numbers are interrogated, the Dragons discover hidden artistic talents when a mindfulness art business is pitched, an optical inventor hopes to have investment in sight with his anti-glare eyewear and a husband-and-wife team have the needs of NHS workers at the heart of their skin-care business. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The semi-finals continue as the second group of surviving amateurs cook a dish for John Torode and Gregg Wallace that they would have on the menu of their own restaurant, pop-up or featured in their cookbook. In an unexpected twist, the contestants discover that they are also presenting their food to top chef Marcus Wareing. After two leave the contest, the remaining three head to The Game Bird restaurant at The Stafford Hotel to work with culinary director Ben Tish, serving the restaurant's staff. No one knows the food better than they do and expectations are high of the contenders

programme.title: The Accountant (2016)
programme.desc: High-functioning autistic number-cruncher Christian Wolff moonlights as a money launderer for the mob. However, the Treasury Department start closing in on him, so he decides to take on a legitimate client in the form of a robotics company. As he works through their books, he uncovers a discrepancy involving millions of dollars. Thriller, starring Ben Affleck, Anna Kendrick and JK Simmons

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: New crimefighting tactics and technology, including a look at cameras with artificial intelligence. Plus, a burglar is caught breaking into an elderly woman's house

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don welcomes spring at Longmeadow with plenty of changes in the garden to share. As well as getting on with some essential pruning, he lifts and divides perennials, starts sowing chillies and begins growing this year's vegetables. Plus, Carol Klein shares the highlights of the plants that shine out in winter at Bressingham Gardens in Norfolk

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Rudolph Walker appeals on behalf of the Stroke Association, a charity working to help people rebuild their lives after stroke

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold presents highlights and analysis from the opening round of matches in the Super League season, including Castleford Tigers v Warrington Wolves, Hull FC v Huddersfield Giants and Leigh Centurions v Wigan Warriors

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: The owners of a traditional Edwardian end-of-terrace, a Victorian terrace brought crashing into the 21st century, and a terrace that has been stretched to its limit all compete

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Jan needs to know who the father of her twins is, and Woody has a dark secret about his wedding. Shay is relieved to have Ike back, but the boat accident has taken its toll on him

programme.title: Nature's Great Events
programme.desc: David Attenborough describes the annual surge of more than 100 million sardines along South Africa's east coast as inshore waters cool with the arrival of winter. This marine spectacle provides a feast for competing predators including dolphins, whales, sharks and seabirds, but the sardine run has become less predictable in recent years - posing a threat to the creatures that depend on it to survive

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham examines the senses, anatomy and physiology of bats to understand what enables the creatures to thrive in surprising places. Tiny hairs on their wings give them a detailed air-flow map during flight, while heat sensors on the nose of vampire bats allow them to search out the most blood-rich areas of a prey's body, and iron oxide particles in the bat brain may act as a compass, enabling them to find the most direct route back to the roost

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts Caroline Hawley and Nick Hall are on hand at the City Hall in Hull, hoping to find a selection of objects to take to auction. Caroline is delighted to find a painting by local artist Steven Dews and Nick is bedazzled by some iconic 1970s jewellery. Presenter Paul Martin visits the Wilberforce House Museum in Hull's Old Town to hear the story of Hull-born William Wilberforce, the great anti-slave trade campaigner

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley, Matt Forde, Fred MacAulay and Judy Murray go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: Loved-but-unused treasures are transformed into contemporary and useful items by a team of expert crafters. This time, Xinhua arrives at the workshop with a Chinese dowry box, which she now uses to keep her make-up, and carpentry expert Shelley and metal specialist John offer radically different designs to choose from. Elsewhere, other items include an old family tent and a single bowling pin

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: For the fourth leg of their journey, Dave Myers and Si King travel from Amarillo, Texas, to Albuquerque in New Mexico. In Amarillo, the pair visit the oldest burger joint in the city, before riding to Palo Duro Canyon to explore a ghost town. Finally arriving in Albuquerque, they join residents to watch a game at the local ballpark and bake a chili chocolate pie to celebrate their cross-country adventure

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Another host of leading food experts take Fred Sirieix to their favourite restaurants to explore what makes them so special. In this opening episode, Nadiya Hussain takes Fred to visit some of her beloved eateries around Yorkshire. Whilst on their journey, the pair sample traditional bakes, world-class cheese and spicy samosas

programme.title: The Detectives: Fighting Organised Crime
programme.desc: When a man is gunned down on a busy residential street in Manchester, a team of specialist GMP detectives race to identify the masked men responsible

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Sindhu Vee hosts this episode of the comedy showcase recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, featuring stand-up performances by New York-based Edinburgh comedy award winner Alex Edelman, and at the Edinburgh Festival Fringe Comedians' Choice Award winner Lou Sanders

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: Chris takes a look at nature's best navigators, with mule deer building up complicated memory maps of their landscapes so they don't get lost, while elephants rely on the knowledge of the herd's matriarch to find precious waterholes. Dung beetles plot routes by reading the stars, while the mantis shrimp has one of the most complex navigation systems in the natural world. For many species of fish and birds, there's safety in numbers and they often travel in vast shoals and flocks - but who leads the way, and how do they decide?

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to the Lake District for an emotional reunion with their own dads' old cars. Elsewhere, Chris heads out on track to test Lamborghini's most powerful car ever, the hybrid Sian

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace present the latest heat, as five more hopefuls get their first chance to win an apron by cooking their Signature Dish - something that tells John and Gregg about who they are as a cook and how good they can become. After tasting all five plates, John and Gregg pick their favourite dishes, and those cooks will earn themselves not only a MasterChef apron but also a place in the next round

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon and his team travel to rural Oxfordshire to meet Asiyla and her family and help transform an old sailing boat into unique glamping accommodation.

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: Footage from a police body camera helps convict a violent offender who attacked a policewoman and the owners of a wine bar use facial recognition cameras to tackle pickpocketing

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: As semi-final week kicks off, the four remaining designers are challenged to transform two restaurants in Hebden Bridge. Judge Michelle Ogundehin reveals that the competitors will be working in pairs, with each designer responsible for a single floor in the restaurant. Who will make it to the final?

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz, with Dubem, Sarah, and Melissa choosing between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three wildcard properties enter the contest in Leicester - a 100-year-old Dutch Barge, a modern mansion and a 'Scandinavian barn'-inspired rebuild

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Woody ropes George in to help him with a secret mission. Meanwhile, Tracy gets Katie to join the teaching staff. Comedy drama, starring Erik Thomson

programme.title: Nature's Great Events
programme.desc: The migration of elephant herds across Botswana's Kalahari Desert during the annual flooding of the Okavango Delta, which turns 4,000 square miles of arid plains into a maze of wetlands, setting the scene for great clashes between animals in search of water. Resourceful elephants make the most of stagnant pools and waterholes guarded by lions, while hippos converge on prime territories to do battle. David Attenborough narrates

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham delves beneath a snake's skin to discover what has made them some of the most successful predators on Earth, examining how their simple body plan hides remarkable adaptations that enable them to rival their limbed, winged and finned counterparts. The host reveals the variety of ways in which snakes use their bodies, and explores how they use their senses to hunt

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team value antiques at Wrest Park, Bedfordshire, as experts Christina Trevanion and David Harper take a look at the collectibles brought in by the public. Paul also travels to Hemel Hempstead to learn about Frogmore Paper Mill

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley, Matt Forde, Fred MacAulay and Judy Murray test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: Loved-but-unused treasures are transformed into contemporary and useful items by a team of expert crafters. This time, Interior design expert Rob and fine furniture maker Daisy are presented with a sailing club's training boat which has been damaged beyond repair. Other items include a musician's first ever trombone, and an old coffee table

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: Dave and Si cross the state of Arizona in search of the Wild West and make a small detour from Route 66, starting in Monument Valley, to learn more about this striking place. They're invited by a local Navajo police officer to eat with his family at a traditional Navajo barbecue, where a sheep is butchered, grilled and eaten alongside some traditional fry bread. Still riding in the Navajo Nation, the bikers stop at Tsaile College to learn more about rodeo and cook a lamb stew with corn dumplings in homage of the Navajo people they met

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Moving into the Scotland heats, chefs Roberta Hall, Amy Elles, Stuart Ralson and Scott Smith present their canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by British invention and innovation. Veteran chef and Michelin-starred Tom Brown, a seafood specialist, tastes their canapes, which range from a cheese tart to a duck liver parfait

programme.title: The Terror
programme.desc: Crozier and Fitzjames try to lead the men in search of safety, and form a closer bond as revelations about their past come to light. As the trek across the ice continues, deaths under mysterious circumstances lead to paranoia, and some of the crew consider mutiny

programme.title: The Terror
programme.desc: The sailors prepare to continue their journey south in the aftermath of the recent attack, despite having sustained considerable losses. The trek is proving too much for Fitzjames, whose health deteriorates severely. Back in London, Lady Jane enlists the aid of Charles Dickens in financing a rescue expedition

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Palermo in northern Sicily, a city echoing Arab, North African, French, Spanish and Italian influences, making its cuisine unique and irresistible. Pine nuts, raisins, almonds and golden breadcrumbs are combined with flavours of sun-ripened tomatoes, lemons and the finest olive oil to create a range of unforgettable dishes. Rick witnesses locals celebrating a festival in honour of Palermo's patron saint Rosalia, and receives a cookery lesson from a real-life duchess. At home he cooks Marsala chicken, a Sicilian classic served in UK bistros since the 1960s

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Black Power: A British Story of Resistance
programme.desc: A look at the 1960s and '70s through the eyes of the young black people who refused to take racism as a part of the natural order of things in the UK. The documentary shows how the civil rights struggle in America inspired activists to take a stand, and features rare archive of Martin Luther King, Malcolm X and Stokely Carmichael's activities in Britain, as well as footage of leading figures in the movement in the UK including Altheia Jones-LeCointe, Darcus Howe and Roy Sawh

programme.title: The Secret Science of Sewage
programme.desc: Dr George McGavin, Dr Zoe Laughlin and a team of world-leading scientists explore the biology, chemistry, natural history and social history of faeces from a TV-base camp like no other - within the pools, tanks and bowels of one of the UK's largest and most technologically advanced sewage works

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: The cultural historian heads to the vast Konya Plain of Turkey, tracking a young explorer who found the world's oldest city and rewrote the beginning of civilisation - until his reputation was destroyed by scandal

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Contestants prepare their Signature Dish, and after tasting all five plates, judges Gregg Wallace and John Torode award not only a place in the quarter-finals for two cooks, but also a MasterChef apron. The remaining trio take part in the Favourite Ingredient Challenge. The stakes are high as at the end, one contender will be sent home. Arbitrators in this edition include 2007 champion Steven Wallis and 2006 finalist Dean Edwards

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: In the Cotswolds, Simon Parfett and his team meet Tammy and Colin. Wanting to spend more time together as a family and create extra income, they decided to buy a horsebox which they could rent out to festival-goers. Alas, things didn't go to plan, so Simon and company come to the rescue

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: After an elderly couple's home is broken into, a police officer looks at burglary patterns in the area and predicts where and when the culprits are likely to strike again. Only this time, he's waiting for them

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: The presenter introduces animals that build structures and adapt the environment for their own benefit, creating safe places to sleep and raise young or traps to catch prey. The programme examines how the huge dams built by beavers can completely transform the surrounding landscape, the function of the different types of silk spiders are capable of producing, and how leafcutter ants build underground gardens where they farm their own food

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three semi-detached properties in Leicester - including a shabby chic family home, and an Edwardian property with some rather eclectic interiors - are in the spotlight

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: The judges - a group of knowledgeable property-obsessed 'nosey neighbours' - return to Leicester to give the winning homes from each category a second look, getting a fresh insight into the houses before deciding which one they like the best. Narrated by Marcus Garvey

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Woody and George's secret operation is now a team effort, including Shaun, who thinks he has invented the perfect cover for a trip to Australia without Tracy finding out the truth

programme.title: Nature's Great Events
programme.desc: How the growth of phytoplankton along the coastal waters of Alaska and British Columbia attracts a vast array of marine life to feast on billions of herring. Footage includes humpback whales migrating from Hawaii as well as Steller sea lions, dolphins and killer whales that make the long and arduous journey toward the abundant fish, risking malnutrition and attacks from predators along the way. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham investigates what enables birds of prey to rule the aerial roost, and reveals that their ability to dominate their fellow birds in terms of strength, manoeuvrability and phenomenal speed is down to a combination of anatomical and physiological adaptations. He explains the inner workings of the bald eagle's ratchet talons, and how sharp eyes and a gyroscopic head enable the goshawk to keep its sight firmly fixed on both its prey and its surroundings

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Claire Rawle and Adam Partridge appraise collectables that reflect local Kent character at Chiddingstone Castle. Meanwhile, presenter Paul Martin visits Anne Boleyn's family home of Hever Castle to reveal what influenced her rise to power

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley, Matt Forde, Fred MacAulay and Judy Murray test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: A post box used in a wedding ceremony and a worn out but much-loved armchair are among the items transformed into beautiful and functional items by the skilled team of remakers

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King mount their bikes for the final leg of their epic journey along the American road. They begin in Las Vegas, where they learn how to make apparently the best mashed potato in the world - inspiring them to cook a massive lobster and prawn cocktail. Back on the road they finally reach California, where they follow the path of the 49ers during the gold rush and learn about cooking with marijuana - which is perfectly legal in Los Angeles. They also enjoy the best tacos they have ever eaten at a taco festival, before reaching Santa Monica pier - the end of their trip

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs are halfway through their heat and the pressure is mounting. Each wants to represent Scotland at a banquet celebrating British invention and innovation. For mains, one chef honours Logie Baird's invention of television with an upmarket take on a TV dinner, and another celebrates Scotland's successful cloning of Dolly The Sheep. A third cooks an ambitious multi-layered pie of beef and chicken with a beef consomme. Presenter Andi Oliver also asks the chefs to prepare a pre-dessert or palate cleanser. Only two will proceed to Friday's judging and have a chance to represent Scotland at the regional finals

programme.title: Pandemic 2020
programme.desc: The story of the first year of the pandemic told by people from all over the world. It begins on New Year's Eve 2019, when huge crowds gathered to celebrate. Contributors include Xie, a young food blogger from Wuhan whose wedding took place a few days before her city went into lockdown. As the virus travels to Europe, Dr Amie from Coventry recalls watching in disbelief the horrific scenes of people dying in Italy

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition of the comedy panel game. Sandi Toksvig talks about rocks, rolls and rock 'n' roll, with Bill Bailey, Katy Brand, Eshaan Akbar and Alan Davies

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: A mix of light-hearted gossip and serious analysis about the latest goings on from Westminster and Brussels, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason

programme.title: The Detectives: Fighting Organised Crime
programme.desc: When a man is gunned down on a busy residential street in Manchester, a team of specialist GMP detectives race to identify the masked men responsible

programme.title: DNA Family Secrets
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley helps people answer life-changing questions by examining their DNA. This time, triplets Peter, David and Phillip, who were adopted as young boys, have their DNA analysed by Professor Turi King as they attempt to explore their birth father's heritage. Meanwhile, a woman who was conceived from a sperm donor longs to learn about the other half of her ancestry, and find out if she has any half donor siblings

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The third quarter-final sees four talented heat-winners return to vie for a place in knockout week by participating in the Critic's Test. The challenge - set by infamously hard task-master Jay Rayner - is to prepare a dessert where stone fruit is the star. Gregg Wallace and John Torode preside as the contenders endeavour to take another step closer to being crowned MasterChef Champion 2021

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and the team travel to Wiltshire to meet Anthony and Bianca who have grand plans to turn the shed at the bottom of their garden into a unique B&B

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: The explorer heads to Derwentwater and Borrowdale, a region of England susceptible to extreme weather, where he meets some of the locals who have battled against flooding and learned to live with the conditions. Along the way, Paul tells the story of Millican Dalton, the 'Professor of Adventure', who ran his own expeditions in the Lake District more than 70 years ago. He also heads for the summit of Great Gable, one of the Lake's best-known peaks

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a seascape with a dramatic sky that marks an approaching storm, setting the mood with a canvas painted half black and half white

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: Jim Hacker believes he needs to know everything going on in his department, and is furious to learn Sir Humphrey is withholding information. However, when events take a surprising turn, he starts to understand why sometimes it is better to keep a minister in the dark

programme.title: The Fall and Rise of Reginald Perrin
programme.desc: Reggie changes nationality several times in his search for a new identity, but gradually finds himself drawn back to his old life. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter

programme.title: Treasures of the Anglo-Saxons
programme.desc: Historian Dr Janina Ramirez investigates the development of Anglo-Saxon art. Examining the Sutton Hoo treasures the Franks Casket, the Staffordshire Hoard and the Lindisfarne Gospels, she uncovers the secret codes and symbols that reveal the pagan past and Christian future of the Anglo-Saxon people, and discusses how their artistic development was brought to an end by the Norman invasion of 1066

programme.title: Catching Britain's Killers: The Crimes That Changed Us
programme.desc: The stories of murder investigations and their consequences, which overturned laws, transformed police interrogation and revolutionised forensic detection. It begins with the hunt to find the killer of two teenage girls in 1980s Leicestershire, which led to an extraordinary forensic breakthrough - DNA fingerprinting

programme.title: Lights Up Festival
programme.desc: Award-winning actress Lisa Dwan stars in this one-woman play, written by Colm Toibin, which recounts Sophocles' tragedy of Antigone from the viewpoint of her sister, Ismene

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a seascape with a dramatic sky that marks an approaching storm, setting the mood with a canvas painted half black and half white

programme.title: Catching Britain's Killers: The Crimes That Changed Us
programme.desc: The stories of murder investigations and their consequences, which overturned laws, transformed police interrogation and revolutionised forensic detection. It begins with the hunt to find the killer of two teenage girls in 1980s Leicestershire, which led to an extraordinary forensic breakthrough - DNA fingerprinting

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Coniston Water isn't the biggest of Cumbria's lakes - but it's one of the most iconic and the place where Donald Campbell died trying to break the world water speed record. Paul meets Campbell's daughter Gina and crew member Robbie Robinson, who was there on the fateful day in January 1967 when Bluebird somersaulted and crashed on the water. The explorer also enters a Lakeland bike ride with a difference before ending his visit with a spot of rock climbing at Dow Crag before the ascent of one of the area's best known hills, The Old Man of Coniston

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene of bright green grassy forest, through which a smooth crystal river flows quietly

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: An examination of the dark history behind Britain's fascination with murder, beginning with historian and curator Lucy Worsley exploring how notorious killings were transformed into popular entertainment in the first half of the 19th century

programme.title: Murder in Soho: Who Killed Freddie Mills?
programme.desc: With access to eight hours of previously unseen home movies, this documentary offers an intimate portrayal of boxing hero Freddie Mills, who rose from the humble surroundings of the fairground boxing booth to become light-heavyweight champion of the world.In July 1965 he was found shot dead in the back seat of his car. Fifty years after his death his relatives still challenge the coroner's verdict of suicide, maintaining he was murdered

programme.title: Lights Up Festival
programme.desc: Drama by David Ireland, filmed at the Lyric Theatre, Belfast. A sharp-tongued cleaner relives painful and harrowing episodes in a showdown with her past. Starring Abigail McGibbon, Patrick Jenkins and David Pearse

programme.title: Brotherhood: The Inner Life of Monks
programme.desc: Documentary about Mount St Bernard Abbey, a monastery with a population of just 25 monks, half of whom are over 80, and which will soon be home to the first Trappist brewery in the UK - a venture that is essential to the brotherhood's survival. The monks reflect on spirituality, ageing and the end of life, as the number of burials in the abbey graveyard continues to grow

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene of bright green grassy forest, through which a smooth crystal river flows quietly

programme.title: Wild
programme.desc: Dean Burman was a painter-decorator, but his dream was to be a wildlife cameraman. This film shares his amateur footage of pike in Leicester, which he collected over many years

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Remote Eskdale is the next destination in the explorer's trek through the Lake District, enjoying the landscapes and meeting its people. The corner of the western Lakes he focuses on is off the normal tourist track, yet boasts England's highest mountain. However, before making the tough climb to the summit of Scafell Pike, Paul meets the folk who run one of the country's most scenic railways, has a close encounter with a vulture, and gets to grips with the locals in a spot of Cumberland wrestling

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a distant waterfall and a bubbling stream against the backdrop of a tumultuous sky

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small

A chance encounter with old friend Stewie Brannon ruins Siegfried's chances of landing a plum job with the county horse racing board

programme.title: The Third Man (1949)
programme.desc: American writer Holly Martins goes to post-Second World War Vienna at the invitation of his old friend Harry Lime, only to find he has died. However, the author finds the circumstances surrounding his death fail to add up and goes in search of the truth - but is unprepared for the ugly secrets he uncovers. Carol Reed's thriller, written by Graham Greene, starring Joseph Cotten, Orson Welles, Alida Valli and Trevor Howard

programme.title: Brighton Rock (2010)
programme.desc: A young criminal in 1960s Brighton is caught on camera killing a man. The evidence falls into the hands of a naive waitress, so the icy murderer seduces her in a bid to ensure her silence. Crime drama based on Graham Greene's novel, starring Sam Riley, Andrea Riseborough, Helen Mirren, Andy Serkis and John Hurt

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Treasures
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank visits Mali and Egypt, where he examines masks said to connect the land of the living with that of the dead, admires the paintings of the Dogon, and learns the story of reputedly the most beautiful woman in history. He also explores the Great Pyramid, then journeys across the desert of Libya before concluding this leg of the expedition with a trip down the Nile

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Jahawi is an underwater camera operator who is passionate about preserving the coastline of Kenya, his home country. The ocean is home to a whole host of marine wildlife, but one species here is particularly under threat: dolphins. This fun and curious mammal is facing a number of issues, as their habitat is being destroyed and the fish they eat are being depleted. There is also significant human activity on this coastline, which is damaging the marine life. However, Lamu has stood the test of time and is a marine sanctuary for them, with a new port being built and things about to change. Working with the Kenyan Marine Mammal Network, Jahawi is hoping to get the area protected as a marine reserve before it’s too late. It doesn’t take long for Jahawi to find the resident bottle-nosed dolphins as they enjoy swimming at the bow of the boat, and he soon gets into the water with the dolphins to get a closer look at these magnificent creatures. here is one species of dolphin he is hoping to

programme.desc: The lives of two young brothers living on the Island of Fofoa in Tonga. programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Megan is a wildlife expert who campaigns for animal rights and has a passion for shark conservation. Her mission is to look into the threats facing sharks in British waters and around the globe, from the deadly fin trade to industrial fishing, which have caused shark population numbers to fall dramatically in recent decades. One species she is keen to know more about is the blue shark, millions of which are caught each year for their fins, which can fetch thousands of dollars in Asia and have also ended up in Chinese restaurants in the UK. Megan also investigates the story behind a recent report that one of the nation’s favourite takeaways – fish and chips – has been linked to a species of shark which is listed as globally vulnerable and is on the brink of extinction in British waters. Megan meets up with marine biologists and shark conservationists to find out more about the dangers to British sharks, and explores ways in which sharks and other fish species can be protected. programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Malaika is a wildlife film-maker and conservationist. Growing up in India inspired her to want to save the wildlife on her doorstep. She has always been fascinated by elephants and her mission is to investigate why these beautiful animals are is in such danger. The Indian elephant is classified as endangered, and Malaika learns that the biggest threats they face are forest fragmentation and living in captivity. She goes undercover to find out more about elephants in the tourist trade in Indian cities and then visits an elephant hospital run by Wildlife SOS, a charity which rehabilitates elephants rescued from captivity. Some of the elephants here were in chains for over 50 years and now have a chance to enjoy life in the wild. Malaika talks about the importance of being aware of wildlife tourism and encourages us to say no to exploitative elephant rides available on holiday.

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: The lives of two young brothers living on the Island of Fofoa in Tonga. After living through the worst tropical cyclone in memory, Felix and Luca enlist a local conservation charity to help them survey their coral reef to see the effects of the storm and check the long term damage from climate change. Protecting the marine life is vital to keep attracting the migrating whales back every year, not least because the boys' favourite hobby is taking visitors swimming to meet these magnificent animals

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join Matilda, Samuel and their families as they discover the world around them at two months old. Both children head out for the day with their families. Samuel goes on a relaxing walk with his mum and dad in the nearby park, whilst Matilda goes to a different park with her family to feed the ducks and watch the graceful swans. After heading home with their families, Samuel plays in his swing with mum. Matilda has a soothing, warm bath before being breastfed and rocked slowly to sleep. Samuel is also getting tired, so mum and dad run him a warm bath to make sure he gets cleaned up, before giving him his bottle of milk. The next morning, Matilda’s family are all in bed together as she gives Callum a big hug! Samuel is also feeling refreshed and ready to have some cuddles with mum before breakfast.

programme.title: What's On Your Head?

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Diellza's family comes from Kosovo. She makes a traditional recipe of stuffed peppers. Diellza has lovely colourful ingredients for her stuffed peppers such as spring onions, sweetcorn, grated carrot. She also uses cooked rice, chopped tomatoes, parsley, garlic puree and paprika for seasoning. This yummy filling is stuffed into her peppers and placed in the oven to be cooked for her friends. To accompany her meal, Diellza serves her friends a traditional Kosovan drink made of yoghurt and water called kos. Will they enjoy her drink, and will they guess what the special ingredient is?

programme.title: Maddie, the Plants and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie on a journey of discovery to find out about plants around the world. Find out how we can help to look after them and our planet, and why and how they grow and survive in different climates. Maddie explores plants in the rainforest, big and small, and sees how the clever drip-tip leaves work watering the plants beneath them. We’ll see how plants in hot, dry parts of world like the desert thrive in their own special ways, like the cactus plant that stores litres of water in its waxy leaves. And we’ll learn how plants that grow close to home in the UK, can grow in a colder and wetter climate.

Maybe he can try and blend in with the chimney? Or perhaps he could hide under gutters? He’d better find somewhere suitable, and quickly. His owlets are expert seekers!

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join Charlie, Etti and their families as they discover the world around them at three months old and learn what makes them both smile. We join Etti, who is having a fun day out with her family at some local gardens. They see lots of different flowers and animals including a big rabbit statue! But will these make Etti and her sister, Grace, smile? Meanwhile, Charlie and his family are out visiting a zoo where they see lots of different birds. Mum does an impression that makes Charlie smile, so his family use an instant camera to take some lovely family photos - but what will they do with them? After a quick stop at the local park, Etti and Grace decide to stop at a friend’s house to play peek-a-boo together. Grace creates her own happy faces with cut-out features. Finally we see what Charlie and his family were taking photos for - a photo album! What a fun and special way to look back on their family day out.

programme.title: What's On Your Head?

programme.title: Tinpo

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Disa's family comes from the Scandinavian country, Sweden. She is baking kardemummabullar, which is a Swedish recipe for cardamom buns. Disa makes a milk mixture, sweetened with honey, and mixes it into flour to make a dough. She then grinds her special ingredient of cardamom into a powder and adds some into the dough and the rest of it into brown sugar. The dough is flattened, the brown sugar and cardamom mix is spread onto the dough and folded in. The dough is then cut into strips and twisted and wrapped into a bun ready to be baked. Will her friends like the cardamom when it's baked in her kardemummabullar?

programme.desc: Join four-month-old Annie-Rose and Theo as they discover and explore the world around them through movement. Both children start at home playing with their families. Theo tries pushing and rolling a ball back and forth with his brother and sisters, before playing on his activity mat where he can grab and shake his rattle and kick his legs. Meanwhile, Annie-Rose has a go at grabbing colourful balls and reaching for her soft, cuddly bunny. Mum and dad play a game with her with balloons, as she learns to help them move. Later, Theo goes out with mum to a baby yoga class where he stretches his arms and legs to sing-along songs. Annie-Rose attends a swimming class - singing, swishing and splashing in the pool! When both children get back home, Theo’s mum blows big bubbles in the garden to see how many he can pop, whilst Annie-Rose finishes off on a video call with grandma where she can practice her waving.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Jessica's family is from Ukraine, a country found in Eastern Europe. This region is known for a soup called borscht, and Jessica will be making some for her friends. Jessica cooks cabbage and spring onions in oil and then adds them to grated carrot, beetroot, potatoes and a tomato and stock mixture. All of these ingredients are combined in a casserole dish. Once they are stirred together, the casserole dish can be placed in the oven to cook. Jessica wears a traditional Ukrainian outfit and serves drinks in a beautiful drinks dispenser. Borscht is famous for being brightly coloured, so will her friends be able to guess that her special ingredient is beetroot?

programme.desc: In the aftermath of Cameron's comeuppance, Nicky vents her frustrations on a patient - and is forced to ask herself whether she really wants to raise a killer's baby. Reyhan challenges a traumatised Hanssen to tell Sahira the truth, but can he do it knowing it will destroy her? The AAU staff are surprised to see Ange back in scrubs so soon after her ordeal

programme.title: Nightshifters
programme.desc: Stuart has been working night shifts at Tata Steelworks in Port Talbot for almost 20 years, Sian is on the late-night taxi shift in Swansea, while in Cardiff, artist Nathan is melting chocolate in a microwave prior to creating a portrait of Lord Alan Sugar using sweets and confectionery. In this continuing series revealing the hidden world of night-time workers, all three reveal their very different reasons for working at night

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: The return of Kay Mellor's lottery-based drama, this time following the workers at Woodvale Kennels, whose future looks in doubt when the owners announce the business is being sold to a large corporate chain. The only lifeline for gambling addict Keeley is the work's lottery, so when she checks the ticket with local newsagent Frank, she is ecstatic to learn they have won £500. But the team soon have reason to believe Frank may have made a mistake. Now all they need to do is find him. Starring Neil Morrissey, Katherine Rose Morley, Taj Atwal and Emily Head

programme.title: Match of the Day Wales
programme.desc: Wales v Czech Republic. Jason Mohammad presents highlights of the World Cup qualifier at Cardiff City Stadium. Studio guests include former Wales international Joe Ledley and ex-Wales manager Jayne Ludlow

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Documentary highlighting how British Jews from across the faith celebrate life's big milestones, from birth to coming of age, marriage to the end of life. A 12-year-old prepares for his bar mitzvah, a couple marry in Spain, a modern ceremony they have adapted according to their beliefs, and at Bushey Cemetery in Hertfordshire, a woman describes her role as a volunteer who does the tahara, the ritual washing of the deceased before burial

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: A look at how British Hindus mark life's big milestones. In south London, Bhumi and Chirag are marking the birth of their six-day-old son with a traditional ceremony at home, inviting the gods to write his future. programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Wales: Land of the Wild
programme.desc: An epic journey following Wales's rivers, from their birth in the mountains to the sea, discovering the hidden secrets of wildlife. Super slow-motion cameras film peregrine falcons on the hunt and reveal why their prey, the unsuspecting red grouse, are often able to evade the fastest predator on Earth. Dragonflies' amazing aerial acrobatics are slowed down 400 times their normal speed and red squirrels are captured as they leap through the trees. In a city park, pigeons hidden superpowers are laid bare, and in an upland garden a subtle hierarchy is at play as birds gather around a feeder

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook heads to Snowdonia to hear the real-life Romeo and Juliet story of Vincenzo Algieri and Betty Evans - two star-crossed lovers who, despite being on opposite sides of the Second World War, found love together against all the odds. From different cultures and speaking different languages, Vincenzo and Betty bonded over food - traditional Italian dishes the family still treasures to this day. Grandson Huw has some clues of the tale the lies within his family history, but hopes Michela's specialist knowledge can help fill in some of the blanks

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley hosts the property-based guessing game in which four people claim to own the same house, while a celebrity panel try work out which one is telling the truth. This week, regulars Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love, Jamali Maddix are joined by Rob Rinder to find the real Michael. From the first sight of the four players the panel are instantly thrown by a familiar face, while the path to the truth is obscured by another Michael's inability to remember anything about his own life

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Bethan Rhys Roberts presents the weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Struggling vlogger Liam continues his mission to become an online influencer by creating a social media-friendly travel experience. Spoof documentary, starring Liam Williams, Tim Key, Arnab Chanda and Emma Sidi

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam tries to make sense of influencers by visiting mega-vlogger Millipede, who has been announced to appear in a West End musical - despite not being able to sing. Liam also attempts to save a struggling art gallery by trying to appeal to a younger audience.

programme.title: The Surrogates
programme.desc: Maddie, Alex and Richard give themselves six months to get pregnant, but as the deadline approaches, Maddie feels low and the trio begin to doubt their future as a surrogacy team. Single man David has his embryo transferred into Faye, and the pair navigate the emotional complexities of the surrogate-parent relationship

programme.title: Football Abuse: The Ugly Side of the Beautiful Game
programme.desc: Reporter Mark Daly explores allegations of a cover-up connected to a notorious paedophile scandal that rocked the youth football circuit in Scotland in the past. He also hears accounts from former footballers who talk for the first time about the abuse they allegedly suffered as boys in the sport

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: After long and demanding careers in the emergency services, a hitherto busy couple are after a change of scenery in the Norfolk countryside. Jonnie Irwin guides them on their rural property tour, showing them some homes available for their £900,000 budget. Jonnie also pays a visit to 15th-century manor house Oxburgh Hall and learns the tricks of the trade used for its 21st-century restoration

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Glaswegian friends Andrew and Stephen have very different experiences of cooking - Andrew cooks mostly for his family and is hoping to get a bit more experimental, whereas Stephen loves to throw dinner parties for friends. Rylan Clark-Neal presides as the two compete, with chefs Romy Gill and Ellis Barrie helping the pair pull out all the stops to create some amazing dishes

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea tackle the motor and body of a hand-made toy racing car, while leather expert Suzie Fletcher get to grips with a leather bowls bag in need of restoration to pass onto the fifth generation of a sports-mad family. Wood expert Will Kirk works his magic on an intricately carved box from the Far East. Its owner's mother bought it with her when she came to Europe from Singapore as a young bride

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: With her hearing looming, Stacey has a fraught final showdown with Martin, while Jean reassures her daughter she will take care of everything at home. Kathy and Kim question Bobby as he waits for his second date, fully embracing his more confident new persona. Mick learns that other survivors have come forward, hopeful that they will be able to give evidence instead of him

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: The business ideas contest moves to BBC One, with entrepreneurs Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies eager to strike more potentially lucrative deals. In this first episode, a pair of tea entrepreneurs find themselves in hot water when their numbers are interrogated, the Dragons discover hidden artistic talents when a mindfulness art business is pitched, an optical inventor hopes to have investment in sight with his anti-glare eyewear and a husband-and-wife team have the needs of NHS workers at the heart of their skin-care business. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The semi-finals continue as the second group of surviving amateurs cook a dish for John Torode and Gregg Wallace that they would have on the menu of their own restaurant, pop-up or featured in their cookbook. In an unexpected twist, the contestants discover that they are also presenting their food to top chef Marcus Wareing. After two leave the contest, the remaining three head to The Game Bird restaurant at The Stafford Hotel to work with culinary director Ben Tish, serving the restaurant's staff. No one knows the food better than they do and expectations are high of the contenders

programme.title: The Accountant (2016)
programme.desc: High-functioning autistic number-cruncher Christian Wolff moonlights as a money launderer for the mob. However, the Treasury Department start closing in on him, so he decides to take on a legitimate client in the form of a robotics company. As he works through their books, he uncovers a discrepancy involving millions of dollars. Thriller, starring Ben Affleck, Anna Kendrick and JK Simmons

programme.title: Election Scotland 2021 - Leaders' Debate
programme.desc: As the campaign begins, the leaders of the five larger political parties debate the big issues ahead of the Holyrood election on May 6. Presented by Sarah Smith, with a virtual audience from across Scotland

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: The return of Kay Mellor's lottery-based drama, this time following the workers at Woodvale Kennels, whose future looks in doubt when the owners announce the business is being sold to a large corporate chain. The only lifeline for gambling addict Keeley is the work's lottery, so when she checks the ticket with local newsagent Frank, she is ecstatic to learn they have won £500. But the team soon have reason to believe Frank may have made a mistake. Now all they need to do is find him. Starring Neil Morrissey, Katherine Rose Morley, Taj Atwal and Emily Head

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: In the aftermath of Cameron's comeuppance, Nicky vents her frustrations on a patient - and is forced to ask herself whether she really wants to raise a killer's baby. Reyhan challenges a traumatised Hanssen to tell Sahira the truth, but can he do it knowing it will destroy her? The AAU staff are surprised to see Ange back in scrubs so soon after her ordeal

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Documentary highlighting how British Jews from across the faith celebrate life's big milestones, from birth to coming of age, marriage to the end of life. A 12-year-old prepares for his bar mitzvah, a couple marry in Spain, a modern ceremony they have adapted according to their beliefs, and at Bushey Cemetery in Hertfordshire, a woman describes her role as a volunteer who does the tahara, the ritual washing of the deceased before burial

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: A look at how British Hindus mark life's big milestones. In south London, Bhumi and Chirag are marking the birth of their six-day-old son with a traditional ceremony at home, inviting the gods to write his future. Meanwhile, when 18-year-old Samahit leaves for university, his family travel with him to bless his new home at the halls of residence

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sisters Linda Atkinson and Pauline Murphy from Merseyside arrive with their mother's pride and joy - a chair created for the investiture ceremony of the Prince of Wales in 1969. One of 4,000 made and later auctioned off to the general public, it is now in need of urgent attention. Others items in need of restoration are an air-powered toy car, an RNLI donation box and a hand-tailored bear used as a Second World War mascot. Presented by Jay Blades
