programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: The return of Kay Mellor's lottery-based drama, this time following the workers at Woodvale Kennels, whose future looks in doubt when the owners announce the business is being sold to a large corporate chain. The only lifeline for gambling addict Keeley is the work's lottery, so when she checks the ticket with local newsagent Frank, she is ecstatic to learn they have won £500. But the team soon have reason to believe Frank may have made a mistake. Now all they need to do is find him. Starring Neil Morrissey, Katherine Rose Morley, Taj Atwal and Emily Head

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Jim Fitzpatrick chairs a topical debate, in which a panel of studio guests face tough questions on the big issues of the week, posed directly by audience members

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Documentary highlighting how British Jews from across the faith celebrate life's big milestones, from birth to coming of age, marriage to the end of life. A 12-year-old prepares for his bar mitzvah, a couple marry in Spain, a modern ceremony they have adapted according to their beliefs, and at Bushey Cemetery in Hertfordshire, a woman describes her role as a volunteer who does the tahara, the ritual washing of the deceased before burial

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: A look at how British Hindus mark life's big milestones. In south London, Bhumi and Chirag are marking the birth of their six-day-old son with a traditional ceremony at home, inviting the gods to write his future. Meanwhile, when 18-year-old Samahit leaves for university, his family travel with him to bless his new home at the halls of residence

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman struggles to cope after Miss Watson's death, while Bradley goes to extreme lengths to find her father's killer. Prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, starring Vera Farmiga, Freddie Highmore and Nicola Peltz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Sally Nugent and Dan Walker present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits Burton upon Trent, Staffordshire, to see a commercial space, and in Kent meets a couple who are returning to property development after taking a few years out. Dion Dublin meets a husband and wife team at a split-level flat in London

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: After an elderly couple's home is broken into, a police officer looks at burglary patterns in the area and predicts where and when the culprits are likely to strike again. Only this time, he's waiting for them

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Special Operations Paramedics are called to the scene of a road traffic accident near Kinross where a car has flipped off the road and landed in a ditch

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Antiques experts Richard Madley and Jonathan Pratt head to West Yorkshire to help two teams search for interesting - and potentially profitable - items at a fair at Wetherby Racecourse. Presenter Charlie Ross also pays a visit to the Bowes museum in Co Durham

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruhma supports one couple from the start to the end of their pregnancy journey - but she seems unable to admit when she needs help herself. Starring Bharti Patel

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah goes property shopping in rural Devon with a couple who have a £350,000 budget and hope to find a home that has both a workshop and an art studio

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Today is a family affair as Lauren and her aunt Jayne compete, hoping to learn new vegan and fish recipes from chefs Jeremy Pang and Romy Gill. Presented by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts repair a small musical table of great sentimental value, a Land Army doll made by the owner's mother and a pair of worn-out driving gloves

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Suzie Lee's Home Cook Heroes
programme.desc: Suzie is on a mission to help viewers cook with confidence. With spring in the air, she creates an Easter feast, starting with Cantonese steamed black bean and ginger salmon. After a visit to Millbank Farm in Co Down, she serves up a Sunday roast of slow-cooked lamb with rich potato dauphinoise, uses the leftovers for lamb kebabs and tops it off with an Easter chocolate cake

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sisters Linda Atkinson and Pauline Murphy from Merseyside arrive with their mother's pride and joy - a chair created for the investiture ceremony of the Prince of Wales in 1969. One of 4,000 made and later auctioned off to the general public, it is now in need of urgent attention. Others items in need of restoration are an air-powered toy car, an RNLI donation box and a hand-tailored bear used as a Second World War mascot. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley hosts the property-based guessing game in which four people claim to own the same house, while a celebrity panel try work out which one is telling the truth. This week, regulars Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love, Jamali Maddix are joined by Rob Rinder to find the real Michael. From the first sight of the four players the panel are instantly thrown by a familiar face, while the path to the truth is obscured by another Michael's inability to remember anything about his own life

programme.title: World Cup Qualifier
programme.desc: Northern Ireland v Bulgaria. Stephen Watson presents highlights of the World Cup qualifier at Windsor Park, as the hosts look for an early boost to their chances of qualifying for Qatar. With commentary by Thomas Kane and analysis by Keith Gillespie

programme.title: This Sporting Life
programme.desc: The ex-footballer reflects on his upbringing in Belfast's Shankill district and his career at the top of his sport during the 1980s. Raised in Belfast's Shankill district, Norman went on to become the youngest player to score for Manchester United's first team, the youngest FA Cup scorer in history and the youngest ever footballer to play in World Cup finals. However, within a decade, his career was cut cruelly short. Speaking to Stephen Watson, Norman reveals the true story behind his sporting life - what it was like to have and then lose it all - and how he managed to rebuild his life. Featuring unseen archive material

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Struggling vlogger Liam continues his mission to become an online influencer by creating a social media-friendly travel experience. Spoof documentary, starring Liam Williams, Tim Key, Arnab Chanda and Emma Sidi

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam tries to make sense of influencers by visiting mega-vlogger Millipede, who has been announced to appear in a West End musical - despite not being able to sing. Liam also attempts to save a struggling art gallery by trying to appeal to a younger audience.

programme.title: The Surrogates
programme.desc: Maddie, Alex and Richard give themselves six months to get pregnant, but as the deadline approaches, Maddie feels low and the trio begin to doubt their future as a surrogacy team. Single man David has his embryo transferred into Faye, and the pair navigate the emotional complexities of the surrogate-parent relationship

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin visit properties being sold at auction in Wigan, Cumbria and Liverpool, chatting to the new owners about their renovation plans

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: How a brave mother fought back against car thieves as they tried to steal her car from her driveway - with her baby inside. Narrated by Rav Wilding

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Special Operations Paramedics Mark and Leigh are called to a car crash in Livingston, where a patient needs to be fully immobilised after complaining of neck pain. Paramedic Gary and Technician Pete attend a patient in Barrhead who has been coughing up blood and needs urgent assessment. Meanwhile, in Edinburgh, Rapid Response Paramedic John is called to a patient with back pain after a fall who is reluctant to go to hospital

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp is joined by experts Danny Sebastian and David Harper at Nottingham racecourse as two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction. Natasha also goes behind the scenes at the National Justice Museum, which is based in Nottingham

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al reluctantly leads a shared patient consultation, while Bear may be out of his depth. The connection between a radio host, a graphic novelist and a timid man needs to be determined

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: After long and demanding careers in the emergency services, a hitherto busy couple are after a change of scenery in the Norfolk countryside. Jonnie Irwin guides them on their rural property tour, showing them some homes available for their £900,000 budget. Jonnie also pays a visit to 15th-century manor house Oxburgh Hall and learns the tricks of the trade used for its 21st-century restoration

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Glaswegian friends Andrew and Stephen have very different experiences of cooking - Andrew cooks mostly for his family and is hoping to get a bit more experimental, whereas Stephen loves to throw dinner parties for friends. Rylan Clark-Neal presides as the two compete, with chefs Romy Gill and Ellis Barrie helping the pair pull out all the stops to create some amazing dishes

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea tackle the motor and body of a hand-made toy racing car, while leather expert Suzie Fletcher get to grips with a leather bowls bag in need of restoration to pass onto the fifth generation of a sports-mad family. Wood expert Will Kirk works his magic on an intricately carved box from the Far East. Its owner's mother bought it with her when she came to Europe from Singapore as a young bride

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: With her hearing looming, Stacey has a fraught final showdown with Martin, while Jean reassures her daughter she will take care of everything at home. Kathy and Kim question Bobby as he waits for his second date, fully embracing his more confident new persona. Mick learns that other survivors have come forward, hopeful that they will be able to give evidence instead of him

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: The business ideas contest moves to BBC One, with entrepreneurs Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies eager to strike more potentially lucrative deals. In this first episode, a pair of tea entrepreneurs find themselves in hot water when their numbers are interrogated, the Dragons discover hidden artistic talents when a mindfulness art business is pitched, an optical inventor hopes to have investment in sight with his anti-glare eyewear and a husband-and-wife team have the needs of NHS workers at the heart of their skin-care business. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The semi-finals continue as the second group of surviving amateurs cook a dish for John Torode and Gregg Wallace that they would have on the menu of their own restaurant, pop-up or featured in their cookbook. In an unexpected twist, the contestants discover that they are also presenting their food to top chef Marcus Wareing. After two leave the contest, the remaining three head to The Game Bird restaurant at The Stafford Hotel to work with culinary director Ben Tish, serving the restaurant's staff. No one knows the food better than they do and expectations are high of the contenders

programme.title: At Home with the Gettys
programme.desc: Easter 2021 sees the 20th anniversary of the release of In Christ Alone, Keith Getty's most famous hymn. Keith and his wife Kristyn's mission is to spread the word of God - through song. Born and bred in Lisburn and Belfast respectively, the couple split their time between their homes in Northern Ireland and Nashville, while performing and touring across multiple continents. As this documentary reveals, lockfown finds the pair busier than ever, adapting their approach to their music and reinventing their business to continue to engage with their community and their fans

programme.title: The Accountant (2016)
programme.desc: High-functioning autistic number-cruncher Christian Wolff moonlights as a money launderer for the mob. However, the Treasury Department start closing in on him, so he decides to take on a legitimate client in the form of a robotics company. As he works through their books, he uncovers a discrepancy involving millions of dollars. Thriller, starring Ben Affleck, Anna Kendrick and JK Simmons

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: As Norman's obsession with Miss Watson continues, Norma tries to distract his thoughts elsewhere. Meanwhile, Bradley faces problems and needs assistance, and Romero is under pressure to make an arrest. A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, starring Vera Farmiga, Freddie Highmore and Max Thieriot

programme.title: The Treaty of Versailles
programme.desc: Professor Margaret MacMillan delivers a Gresham College lecture looking back at the Treaty of Versailles a hundred years later, from 4 June 2019

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: Conservative party historian Lord Lexden charts the career of Andrew Bonar Law from his birth in Canada to Downing Street, via Pembroke Lodge in Kensington

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Commons Home Affairs Committee session on the work of the UK government's Home Office, with evidence from the home secretary Priti Patel and officials, from Wednesday 24 February

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to the Booker prize-winning author Douglas Stuart. His novel, Shuggie Bain, centres on a boy growing up amid poverty, addiction and intolerance in Glasgow. There are deep parallels with his own life. How does he extract so much love from hardship? First shown on the BBC News Channel on 18 February.

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
programme.desc: Statement in the Assembly on updates to the Covid-19 response and social restrictions in Northern Ireland, with first minister Arlene Foster, from Tuesday 16 March

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Treasury select committee session taking evidence on the 2021 Budget from the Chancellor of the Exchequer, Rishi Sunak

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, observes that the pandemic has forced states to confront how people value health, wealth and opportunity. During the first few months of the crisis, most states chose to value human life more than the economic well-being of the nation-state. But if that seems to be changing how do we assess value in this sense? Dr Carney elucidates surprising differences in the financial value put on a human life in different nations - and goes on to argue that this reductionist approach fails to take into account deeper thinking about the worth of human existence. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: The idea of a social contract between the individual and the state is a staple of political philosophy. But what happens when that contract is threatened by forces beyond the control of any government, like a climate crisis or, right now, a global pandemic? Stephen Sackur speaks to Minouche Shafik, director of the London School of Economics and former top official at the World Bank. Is humanity capable of collective action to meet global challenges?

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of a session of the DCMS Committee on the economics of music streaming, with witnesses from Amazon, Apple and Spotify, as well as DCMS minister Caroline Dinenage, from Tuesday 23 February. First shown on BBC Parliament on 27 February.

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Recorded coverage

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: MPs on the Health and Social Care Committee hear from NHS England Chief Executive Sir Simon Stevens and NHS England Chief Operating Officer Amanda Pritchard, from Tuesday 9 March

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his final lecture, Mark Carney turns his attention to climate change, arguing that the roots of our environmental emergency lie in a deeper crisis of values

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of the work of committees set up to investigate aspects of public policy and society

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: HARDtalk: Stephen King Stephen Sackur interviews prolific writer Stephen King. He's written more than 60 books and sold close to 400 million copies. Films like the Shining, Carrie and Stand by Me are all based on his stories. He's been writing horror for half a century, but how has our appetite for fear evolved?

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of the Health and Social Care select committee session on the Health and Social Care White Paper with evidence from the Health Secretary, Matt Hancock, and Jason Yiannikkou, Director of NHS Legislation Programme at Department of Health and Social Care

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: Prime Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of questions in the House of Commons to Prime Minister Boris Johnson

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Joint National Security Strategy Committee session on the UK's national security machinery, with evidence from former prime minister David Cameron and former national security advisor Peter Rickets, from Monday 1 March.

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Former Governor of the Bank of England Mark Carney charts how financial value has come to be held in higher esteem than human value, beginning by assessing what value actually means. How looks at whether the way it is assessed both shapes human values and constrains choices, and looks at how the markets affect society. Introduced by Anita Anand

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Despite losing the presidency and both Houses of Congress, Donald Trump still seems to have a chokehold on the Republican party. So what should Republican anti-Trumpers do now? Do they fight from within, or create a new Conservative Party? Stephen Sackur interviews Evan McMullin, one of their key organisers and strategists

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Former prime minister Tony Blair speaking at the Institute for Government on the last twelve months of the Covid-19 pandemic, its lasting impact and the challenges posed for politicians, from Friday 26 February

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: David Cornock tells the story of how Toynbee Hall and London's East End shaped the politics of Labour prime minister Clement Attlee

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of the Science and Technology select committee session on UK science, research and technology capability and influence in global disease outbreaks with evidence from the Chief Medical Officer for England, Dr Chris Whitty, and the government's Chief Scientific Adviser, Sir Patrick Vallance

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of the Science and Technology select committee session on UK science, research and technology capability and influence in global disease outbreaks with evidence from the Chief Medical Officer for England, Dr Chris Whitty, and the government's Chief Scientific Adviser, Sir Patrick Vallance

programme.title: Scottish First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of questions to First Minister Nicola Sturgeon in the Scottish Parliament

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Commons Science and Technology Committee session on establishing a new UK research funding agency, with evidence from the former adviser to the prime minister Dominic Cummings, and the business secretary Kwasi Kwarteng, from Wednesday 17 March.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Donald Trump's presidency is history. So not surprisingly historians are trying to find some perspective on a president who stress-tested American democracy like no other. Stephen Sackur speaks to Timothy Snyder, one of America's leading historians on totalitarianism and the Holocaust. He believes Trump and his movement brought America face to face with early-stage fascism, but is that pushing historical parallels too far? First shown on the BBC News Channel on 22 February.

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: The debate in the House of Lords on the 2021 Budget, from earlier today.

programme.title: Parliamentary Firsts
programme.desc: Selina Seth presents a short guide to the history of diversity in the House of Commons

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Three former chancellors, Norman Lamont, Alistair Darling and George Osborne, in conversation on 'tax in the real world' at the Institute for Government, from Tuesday 9 March

programme.title: Welsh First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage from the Welsh Assembly as First Minister Mark Drakeford tackles queries on issues affecting life in Wales

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his second Reith Lecture, Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, takes his audience back to the high drama of the financial crisis of 2008, which ended a period when bankers saw themselves as unassailable Masters of the Universe. More than a decade on, how much have the bankers changed their ways? How far has the financial sector changed? Dr Carney says that society must remain vigilant and resist the 'three lies of finance'. If not, it will live with a system that is ill-prepared for the next crisis. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Joint Lords and Commons committee session examining the UK government's review of the Human Rights Act 1998, with evidence from the former president of the UK supreme court Baroness Hale, from Wednesday 3 February

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Stephen Sackur speaks to American psychologist professor Laurie Santos, whose work on the science of happiness has won her an audience of millions far beyond the lecture theatre. With Covid putting enormous pressure on mental health, can we really learn how to be happy?

programme.title: Stormont: From Parliament to Power Sharing
programme.desc: BBC Parliament's Faye Kidd presents a brief history of the Northern Ireland legislature from Parliament to power-sharing

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Debate Coverage of the International Women's Day debate in the House of Commons, from Thursday 11 March.

programme.title: Ten Minute Rule Bill
programme.desc: SNP MP Douglas Chapman introduces his Bill to the House of Commons under the ten minute rule, covering the automated purchase and resale of gaming hardware to tackle the problem of online 'scalping' relating to games consoles, from Wednesday 24 March.

programme.desc: Professor Margaret MacMillan delivers a Gresham College lecture looking back at the Treaty of Versailles a hundred years later, from 4 June 2019

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: An undercover investigation inside a lab analysing thousands of Covid-19 tests a day. Secretly filmed footage reveals a failing service with shoddy practices where staff complain they are under pressure to meet targets despite the lab often running well below capacity. The programme discovers there have been three outbreaks of coronavirus among staff and that social distancing is poorly maintained. Test samples sometimes arrive poorly packaged and labelled, with equipment frequently malfunctioning, leading to contamination of results. The programme also discovers that tests, including some intended to find new variants of coronavirus, have been wrongly discarded or lost

programme.desc: When a sudden urge to fly the Mark IV hits, Penfold leads a confused Danger Mouse to a farm, from which he claims to feel an unexplained magnetic pull

programme.title: Fuine (Home Baking)
programme.desc: Mairi MacRitchie samples a variety of delicious and adventurous treats from Lewis to Skye. She tries cupcakes for adults - delicate little cakes, but with the added kick of gin and tonic, and decadent profiteroles filled with rich chocolate mousse. For a savoury main course she tries a venison pie filled with tender meat, cranberries and chestnuts. From her own kitchen in Uist, Mairi makes her striking walnut cake with a white American frosting and a meringue roulade with a crunchy almond crust filled with fresh raspberries

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: More celebrity interviews and topical debate from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a pub quiz team from Oxfordshire answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot.

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Daniel, Kat, Marcia and Geoff pit their wits against a ruthless quiz genius in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Liv confides in Aaron, and Nicola decides to take matters into her own hands

programme.title: For the Love of Britain
programme.desc: Julie Walters presents a guide to some of the most beautiful sights Northumberland has to offer. Robson Green tries out a very testing sleepover on the Farne islands, while Denise Welch and Carol McGiffin go walking around the Roman Wall. Elsewhere, Ade Edmondson heads to sea in search of the treasures of the deep, and Harry Potter star David Bradley returns to Alnwick castle

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the big-prize quiz, in which another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck

programme.title: Britain's Tiger Kings - On the Trail with Ross Kemp
programme.desc: Part one of two. The presenter meets people who own dangerous and exotic animals in Britain, including an ex-circus trainer who owns five tigers and a man who keeps lions in his back garden. He also meets a couple with a vast menagerie made from scrap, before taking a step inside a lion's cage

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: On Assignment
programme.desc: Geraint Vincent heads to Warsaw to explore how Poland and the EU are increasingly at odds when it comes to the rule of law and human rights, while Dan Rivers is in Georgia speaking to the country's Prime Minister about her plans for transforming one of the former Soviet Union's flagship spa resorts. Emma Murphy travels to California - the prime location for catching a glimpse of grey whales as they make their annual migration up the US West Coast

programme.title: DNA Journey
programme.desc: Eight celebrities prepare to embark on a life changing journey to explore their family history using DNA technology and genealogy. This time, father and son duo Martin and Roman Kemp delve into their family history which spans North London and the North East. As Roman's journey takes him to the British Museum, Martin comes face to face with a past that was never spoken about as he hears tales of female heroism. Later, the pair are shocked to find ancestors leading parallel lives with their own

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Quiz, hosted by Omid Djalili, in which nine contestants are assigned a number between one and nine. They are then whittled down to a final four, and the combination of numbers they are assigned represents the size of the jackpot they play to win in a final round

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy has wedding day nerves, and Aaron tries to get Vinny to open up

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: England v Poland (Kick-off 7.45pm). All the action from both teams' third 2022 FIFA World Cup Group I qualifier, held at Wembley Stadium. These two teams started the campaign as the group favourites and it was England who prevailed 2-0 when the sides last met here in qualification for the 2014 World Cup, with Wayne Rooney and Steven Gerrard scoring the goals. Presented by Mark Pougatch, with analysis by Ashley Cole, Roy Keane and Ian Wright, commentary by Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon, with interviews by reporter Gabriel Clarke

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: International Football Highlights
programme.desc: Mark Pougatch presents action from the latest fixtures, including England v Poland at Wembley Stadium, Wales v Czech Republic at Cardiff City Stadium, Northern Ireland v Bulgaria at Windsor Park and Scotland v Faroe Islands at Hampden Park. Ian Wright, Ally McCoist and Lee Dixon provide the expert analysis

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look at what goes into orange squash and a profile of a family firm making brooms. Plus, an insight into the care that goes into making a Savile Row suit, and how trumpets are handcrafted. Ricky Tomlinson narrates

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Alan Carr's Epic Gameshow
programme.desc: The comedian hosts a revival of classic game show Take Your Pick. A selection of plucky audience members go up against the gong and play the famous Yes/No game as Alan bombards them with hilarious questions. programme.title: DNA Journey
programme.desc: Eight celebrities prepare to embark on a life changing journey to explore their family history using DNA technology and genealogy. As Roman's journey takes him to the British Museum, Martin comes face to face with a past that was never spoken about as he hears tales of female heroism. Later, the pair are shocked to find ancestors leading parallel lives with their own

The host also catches up with some of the couples who found lasting love after meeting on the show

programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking Strictly Come Dancing professional Oti Mabuse, tennis coach Judy Murray and actor-comedian John Thomson to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. One of them will make it through to the Super Catchphrase round, where they could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers featuring celebrity lookalikes, with kids using Brian May's scalp as a pinata, Orlando Bloom showing off his new truss and Mumford & Sons revealing how they keep in touch with the working classes

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, including a birthday girl whose hair catches fire and dogs revealing where they stand on the subject of Europe

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring a tiny archaeologist digging up more than anyone bargained for. Plus, the reason for never playing swingball while going commando, and an eating competition unveils the answer to childhood obesity

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Comedy from Harry Hill, inspired by viewers' home videos. Featuring a toddler who destroys Stonehenge, babysitting tips from a cross-dressing dad, the world's first heavy-petting zoo and a seagull that loves electronic dance music

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter is struck by lightning and hovers on the brink of death. In his state of limbo he experiences flashbacks that make him realise he should pay more attention to his beloved wife. At the same time, he agrees to arrange a date for Death with the girl from the pet shop. Peter Frampton guest stars

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois becomes a martial arts expert and puts her new-found skills to use by chasing away tourists watching the leaves fall in Quahog. Unfortunately her zeal for combat soon infects the entire Griffin household, leading to an all-out family brawl

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: Marcus and Grumio are thrown into turmoil by the sudden departure of Stylax and the arrival of builder Jason. Return of the comedy, with Tom Rosenthal and Ryan Sampson

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: The boys' bar receives a devastating review from the leading food critic in Rome, so Grumio invites her back for a rematch with his experimental new menu. With Annette Badland

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter gets on the wrong side of socially inept gangster Big Fat Paulie, putting Lois in the sights of a hitman. The Griffins visit the Mob boss on the day of his daughter's wedding in the hope that he will call off the killing. Animated comedy, with the guest voice of Michael Chiklis

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan is delighted when Roger offers to help him open a restaurant, but their differences of opinion wreak havoc. Elsewhere, Steve sees an opportunity for romance when the new girl at school invites him over while her parents are out of town

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Francine asks Stan to give Steve 'the talk' when he starts acting out his sexual energy, while the family builds a water slide in the back yard

programme.title: The Stand Up Sketch Show
programme.desc: Luke Kempner, Rachel Jackson, Russell Kane, Dane Baptiste, Luke McQueen, Steen Raskopoulos, Lily Phillips and Tom Parry routines are reconstructed as sketches

programme.title: Don't Hate the Playaz
programme.desc: Maya Jama, Michael Dapaah and London Hughes go up against Lady Leshurr, Natasia Demetriou and NSG as they go head to head in rap battles, dance-offs and freestyling challenges. Jordan Stephens hosts, with help from DJ Shortee Blitz on the decks

programme.title: Iain Stirling's CelebAbility
programme.desc: Iain Stirling hosts as five friends take on Scarlett Moffatt, Liam 'Gatsby' Blackwell, Luke Kempner, Amelia Lily and Arron Crascall in challenges based on unusual abilities

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Matt Edmondson narrates attempts to make records for opening bottles using teeth, restraining motorcycles and catching arrows with bare hands

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: ITV2 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des pushes Samantha to speak to Ritchie about a divorce. Deirdre is remanded on bail. Les grows tired of the protestors. Jim and Gary work on replacing the Rovers' staircase

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily refuses to give up the protest. Ritchie advises Des to get away from Samantha while he can. Zoe threatens Fred with a legal battle if he evicts her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Eric throws his weight around at Home Farm in front of Stella. Laura suggests she and Chris go on holiday. Marlon tries to win Lyn back. Viv is over-friendly with Stella

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon sneaks into Eric's flat and dyes his moustache. Scott tries it on with Kelly again. Chris invites Kathy to dinner at Laura's. Stella gets fed up of Eric

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick awaits the arrival in Aidensfield of a violent criminal recently released from prison, and Kate finds herself cut off by snow when she tries to help a heavily pregnant patient get to hospital. Meanwhile, Greengrass braves the harsh weather himself when his faithful canine companion Alfred goes missing. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid permits Mike's dancer girlfriend to stay for a couple of days, but soon changes his mind when he finds out what sort of performer she really is. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack are assigned by Blakey to take a class of rowdy schoolchildren on their annual outing. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Bob Grant

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack tease Blakey about the obvious attention he is receiving from the canteen girl

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick faces a dilemma after one of his colleagues is suspected of robbery and Greengrass develops a keen interest in a villager's greyhound. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The villagers turn up for the Home Farm party. Marlon dreads having to fulfil Lyn's dare. Roy nearly catches Kelly and Scott together. Biff and Kathy chat about old times

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Stella decides she needs Eric around after all. Kelly is sickened by what she and Scott did. Zak plans Belle's naming ceremony, but Lisa insists on a proper church christening

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick and Leanne are delighted when their wedding confirmation comes through. Samantha feels that Des is going too fast for her. Gary encourages Judy to back to work

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary pushes Judy to ask Fisher for her job back. Roy overhears Nick and Leanne planning their elopement. Ken tells Alma he does not believe that Deirdre is completely innocent

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a well-known business tycoon leads Morse to investigate the man's feuding family, and inadvertently makes him the unwitting target of a hate campaign by the tabloids - a problem compounded by his uneasy relationship with a new chief superintendent. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star alongside Gwen Taylor, Martin Clunes, Alun Armstrong, Anna Massey, Charlotte Coleman and Rupert Graves

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local boxer John Kinsella's successful world title bid stirs up hidden passions among the residents of Midsomer Morchard. As plans to stage a celebratory re-enactment of a Victorian prizefight shape up, jealousy, betrayal and blackmail take a ringside seat, while two suspicious deaths suggest Barnaby has let friendship cloud his judgement. Detective drama, guest starring Phil Daniels (Quadrophenia, EastEnders), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A respected gynaecologist is accused of sexually assaulting a patient during a routine examination, but Brooks, Devlin and Chandler struggle to find any firm evidence and the team becomes divided. Crown Prosecutor Alesha Phillips refuses to accept defeat and takes on formidable defence barrister Phyllis Gladstone. Crime drama, guest starring Derek Riddell and Lesley Manville, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Harriet Walter and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel can't get Gill's remarks about her fling with Will out of her head, so ducks out of attending an awards ceremony with him, fearing their colleagues will believe she's sleeping her way to the top. He finally decides that dating her is too much like hard work and so announces they are no longer an item - a move that leaves Rachel gutted and regretting her behaviour. Janet isn't happy either - it's clear her daughter Elise is about to have her heart broken by Eleanor's son Ade. The duo are soon distracted from their woes by a new case involving the murder of a landlord and his wife who were shot at close range in their pub

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a well-known business tycoon leads Morse to investigate the man's feuding family, and inadvertently makes him the unwitting target of a hate campaign by the tabloids - a problem compounded by his uneasy relationship with a new chief superintendent. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star alongside Gwen Taylor, Martin Clunes, Alun Armstrong, Anna Massey, Charlotte Coleman and Rupert Graves

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Olive becomes convinced Arthur is having an affair with a conductress at the depot. Comedy, with Reg Varney, Anna Karen and Michael Robbins

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack decide to get fit, disturbed to learn that bus crews have a short life expectancy. Comedy, with Reg Varney

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An attempted murder leads Nick to uncover a local landowner's shady past, while reports of illegal boxing bouts keep his fellow coppers on their toes. Meanwhile, Kate discusses the reason for Alex Ferrenby's increasingly erratic behaviour

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Roy is over the moon after his night with Kelly. Eric starts his first day as estate manager. Mandy and Tricia discover Bernice has been lying about her age

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: No one can believe Kelly is serious about Roy. Donna goes back to school and Andy is bullied by his new classmates. Zak and Lisa are at each other's throats

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim and Gary remove the Duckworths' new staircase until they get paid. Jack enjoys having Vera stuck upstairs. Hayley thanks Alma for standing up for her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick and Leanne feel guilty as they leave for Scotland. Alma grows tired of Hayley following her around. Vera gives Jim and Gary a cheque and they replace the staircase

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Leaving behind his beloved Oxford, Morse and a reluctant Lewis set off to investigate the bizarre death of an Englishwoman who was attending a psychotherapy clinic in northern Italy. Their inquiries lead them to the vineyards and villas of Vicenza and the city of Verona - as well as a glamorous opera singer and a convicted fraudster. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star as the detective duo, with Frances Barber, Michael Kitchen, James Grout and Jolyon Baker

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster is invited to the wedding of her former housemaid Gwenda, only to be caught up in a murder mystery involving the groom's family. Geraldine McEwan stars, with Juliet Stevenson, Denis Lawson and Alison Steadman

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: A housewife found choked to death in her own home marks the third strangulation in a month, setting Oxford police on edge. As a manhunt begins in search of a murderer targeting married women, Endeavour looks into the origin of the silk stocking used to kill the latest victim, tracing the garment to its sole supplier in the city. However, as the suspect list narrows and the culprit stalks his prey with increasing desperation, Morse and Thursday find their private lives turned upside down and miss a deeper tragedy unfolding

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A tragic communication error between officers leaves gay police constable Nick Bentley caught in the crossfire of armed drug dealers, with no back-up. Brooks and Devlin follow a lead to a small-time dealer who claims to have seen a second policeman standing nearby, doing nothing while his partner bled to death. The story shocks the detectives and their boss Chandler as they investigate a homophobic religious group at the centre of the incident involving members of their own force. Crime drama, starring Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Harriet Walter

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet voices her concerns to Dodson about Gill's drinking, hoping it can be dealt with amicably, so she's unnerved when her observations are taken more seriously than she intended. Meanwhile, just as Rachel feels like she's getting to grips with the sergeant's job, she makes a fatal error that puts Janet and Chris in danger. The body of a man who disappeared 15 years previously is found on the moors. Inquiries lead to a nearby farm, where the detectives encounter something much more sinister than they could have anticipated

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Leaving behind his beloved Oxford, Morse and a reluctant Lewis set off to investigate the bizarre death of an Englishwoman who was attending a psychotherapy clinic in northern Italy. Their inquiries lead them to the vineyards and villas of Vicenza and the city of Verona - as well as a glamorous opera singer and a convicted fraudster. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star as the detective duo, with Frances Barber, Michael Kitchen, James Grout and Jolyon Baker

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean informs Sid she wants a lover to bring a bit of spice back into her life. Comedy, starring Diana Coupland and Sid James

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack and Stan volunteer Olive as supervisor for the depot nursery. However, when she breaks her glasses and has to go home for a spare pair the clueless duo are left holding the fort. Comedy, starring Reg Varney, Bob Grant and Anna Karen

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan believes living at home is cramping his style and resolves to find his own place. Vintage comedy with Reg Varney and Bob Grant

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster is invited to the wedding of her former housemaid Gwenda, only to be caught up in a murder mystery involving the groom's family. Geraldine McEwan stars, with Juliet Stevenson, Denis Lawson and Alison Steadman

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A relative brings important news, and Nick is puzzled when Kate seems less than pleased to see her. Meanwhile, a wedding is thrown into confusion by a family death. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak finally agrees to a church do for Belle. Scott presses Mandy for info about Kelly and Roy. Bernice confesses all her plans to Mandy and Tricia

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak is not pleased that the rest of the Dingles want Paddy as godfather. Roy plans to propose to Kelly. Donna and Andy make up at school

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick is frustrated by Gail's dislike of Leanne. Hayley is thrilled when Alma invites her to her home. Leanne urges Nick to tell Gail they are married

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne assures Nick that she does not regret marrying him. Curly learns that Hayley's father has died. Des is startled when Samantha packs her bags

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective gains a taste of prison life while investigating the murder of an inmate jailed for a property swindle. The victim's two partners-in-crime are also incarcerated in the same low-security cell block. Morse is determined to find out whether they know anything about the killing, but has to avoid offending the prison authorities while also dealing with a brash subordinate. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with guest appearances by Sean Bean, Richard Wilson, Jim Broadbent, Diana Quick and Steven Mackintosh

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The last run of the detective drama ended with Lewis (Kevin Whately) starting a new life away from the force, but it seems that peace and quiet doesn't suit him - which is just as well, as newly promoted DI Hathaway (Laurence Fox) is struggling to find a sidekick, and within just four weeks is already on to his second sergeant, DS Lizzie Maddox (Angela Griffin). So, Chief Superintendent Innocent (Rebecca Front) decides to reunite the former partners to look into the murder of a neurosurgeon, a case with potential links to the worlds of animal rights and blood sports. In the first of this two-part story, suspicion falls on glamorous widow Erica (Kara Tointon), but it turns out there are plenty of other people in the victim's inner circle who were driven by fear and loathing

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate when the body of missing 10-year-old Jodie Gaines is found in a skip. The pair immediately identify two main suspects - a taxi driver who claims to have dropped the girl off at her guitar lesson, and the teacher who swears she never arrived. The case then takes an unexpected turn, leaving Steel and Phillips with the toughest choices of their careers. Dervla Kirwan guest stars, alongside Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The discovery of slaves on the Pritchard farm has turned a murder inquiry into a multi-agency operation. The enormity of the case brings Dodson to Syndicate 9, keen to establish Gill's state of mind and efficacy as senior officer. Having been the driver involved in the fatal collision with Cal Pritchard, Janet is under investigation and unable to interview the man's widow Evie. Devastated by the consequences of her actions, the detective eventually finds solace in Chris's calm and kind reasoning, but when an incandescent Evie smells alcohol on Gill's breath, she has all the ammunition she needs to discredit the case and bring the DCI down

programme.title: Inspector Morse

John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with guest appearances by Sean Bean, Richard Wilson, Jim Broadbent, Diana Quick and Steven Mackintosh

programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An attempted murder leads Nick to uncover a local landowner's shady past, while reports of illegal boxing bouts keep his fellow coppers on their toes. Meanwhile, Kate discusses the reason for Alex Ferrenby's increasingly erratic behaviour

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Roy is over the moon after his night with Kelly. Eric starts his first day as estate manager. Mandy and Tricia discover Bernice has been lying about her age

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: No one can believe Kelly is serious about Roy. Donna goes back to school and Andy is bullied by his new classmates. Zak and Lisa are at each other's throats

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim and Gary remove the Duckworths' new staircase until they get paid. Jack enjoys having Vera stuck upstairs. Hayley thanks Alma for standing up for her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick and Leanne feel guilty as they leave for Scotland. Alma grows tired of Hayley following her around. Vera gives Jim and Gary a cheque and they replace the staircase

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Leaving behind his beloved Oxford, Morse and a reluctant Lewis set off to investigate the bizarre death of an Englishwoman who was attending a psychotherapy clinic in northern Italy. Their inquiries lead them to the vineyards and villas of Vicenza and the city of Verona - as well as a glamorous opera singer and a convicted fraudster. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star as the detective duo, with Frances Barber, Michael Kitchen, James Grout and Jolyon Baker

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster is invited to the wedding of her former housemaid Gwenda, only to be caught up in a murder mystery involving the groom's family. Geraldine McEwan stars, with Juliet Stevenson, Denis Lawson and Alison Steadman

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: A housewife found choked to death in her own home marks the third strangulation in a month, setting Oxford police on edge. As a manhunt begins in search of a murderer targeting married women, Endeavour looks into the origin of the silk stocking used to kill the latest victim, tracing the garment to its sole supplier in the city. However, as the suspect list narrows and the culprit stalks his prey with increasing desperation, Morse and Thursday find their private lives turned upside down and miss a deeper tragedy unfolding

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A tragic communication error between officers leaves gay police constable Nick Bentley caught in the crossfire of armed drug dealers, with no back-up. Brooks and Devlin follow a lead to a small-time dealer who claims to have seen a second policeman standing nearby, doing nothing while his partner bled to death. The story shocks the detectives and their boss Chandler as they investigate a homophobic religious group at the centre of the incident involving members of their own force. Crime drama, starring Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Harriet Walter

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet voices her concerns to Dodson about Gill's drinking, hoping it can be dealt with amicably, so she's unnerved when her observations are taken more seriously than she intended. Meanwhile, just as Rachel feels like she's getting to grips with the sergeant's job, she makes a fatal error that puts Janet and Chris in danger. The body of a man who disappeared 15 years previously is found on the moors. Inquiries lead to a nearby farm, where the detectives encounter something much more sinister than they could have anticipated

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan believes living at home is cramping his style and resolves to find his own place. Vintage comedy with Reg Varney and Bob Grant

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster is invited to the wedding of her former housemaid Gwenda, only to be caught up in a murder mystery involving the groom's family. Geraldine McEwan stars, with Juliet Stevenson, Denis Lawson and Alison Steadman

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A relative brings important news, and Nick is puzzled when Kate seems less than pleased to see her. Meanwhile, a wedding is thrown into confusion by a family death. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak finally agrees to a church do for Belle. Scott presses Mandy for info about Kelly and Roy. Bernice confesses all her plans to Mandy and Tricia

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak is not pleased that the rest of the Dingles want Paddy as godfather. Roy plans to propose to Kelly. Donna and Andy make up at school

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick is frustrated by Gail's dislike of Leanne. Hayley is thrilled when Alma invites her to her home. Leanne urges Nick to tell Gail they are married

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne assures Nick that she does not regret marrying him. Curly learns that Hayley's father has died. Des is startled when Samantha packs her bags

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective gains a taste of prison life while investigating the murder of an inmate jailed for a property swindle. The victim's two partners-in-crime are also incarcerated in the same low-security cell block. Morse is determined to find out whether they know anything about the killing, but has to avoid offending the prison authorities while also dealing with a brash subordinate. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with guest appearances by Sean Bean, Richard Wilson, Jim Broadbent, Diana Quick and Steven Mackintosh

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The last run of the detective drama ended with Lewis (Kevin Whately) starting a new life away from the force, but it seems that peace and quiet doesn't suit him - which is just as well, as newly promoted DI Hathaway (Laurence Fox) is struggling to find a sidekick, and within just four weeks is already on to his second sergeant, DS Lizzie Maddox (Angela Griffin). So, Chief Superintendent Innocent (Rebecca Front) decides to reunite the former partners to look into the murder of a neurosurgeon, a case with potential links to the worlds of animal rights and blood sports. In the first of this two-part story, suspicion falls on glamorous widow Erica (Kara Tointon), but it turns out there are plenty of other people in the victim's inner circle who were driven by fear and loathing

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate when the body of missing 10-year-old Jodie Gaines is found in a skip. The pair immediately identify two main suspects - a taxi driver who claims to have dropped the girl off at her guitar lesson, and the teacher who swears she never arrived. The case then takes an unexpected turn, leaving Steel and Phillips with the toughest choices of their careers. Dervla Kirwan guest stars, alongside Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The discovery of slaves on the Pritchard farm has turned a murder inquiry into a multi-agency operation. The enormity of the case brings Dodson to Syndicate 9, keen to establish Gill's state of mind and efficacy as senior officer. Having been the driver involved in the fatal collision with Cal Pritchard, Janet is under investigation and unable to interview the man's widow Evie. Devastated by the consequences of her actions, the detective eventually finds solace in Chris's calm and kind reasoning, but when an incandescent Evie smells alcohol on Gill's breath, she has all the ammunition she needs to discredit the case and bring the DCI down

programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as DJ Scott Mills, actress Sunetra Sarker, boxer Anthony Ogogo and TV presenter Johnny Ball pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. In 2019, the airport is having its busiest year to date, and are expecting more than 81 million passengers to fly in and out of the terminals, with its 76,000 staff ready to take on whatever is thrown at them

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Airside officer Ian has a close shave out on the runway. Meanwhile, veteran Border Force officers Glen and Amarjit hunt down smuggled cargo

programme.title: Jack Reacher (2012)
programme.desc: A former army sniper is arrested following a random shooting that leaves five people dead, but protests his innocence. programme.title: On Her Majesty's Secret Service (1969)
programme.desc: While on leave, James Bond prevents a young woman - Teresa 'Tracy' Draco - from committing suicide. Her father is Marc-Ange Draco, the head of a powerful crime syndicate who is impressed by Bond and wants him to protect his daughter by marrying her. In exchange, he offers Bond information that will lead him to master criminal and arch-enemy Ernst Stavro Blofeld, who is intent on destroying world peace by means of a lethal virus unless he receives an international amnesty for his crimes. Spy adventure, starring George Lazenby in his only outing as 007, alongside Telly Savalas, Diana Rigg, Gabrielle Ferzetti and Bernard Lee

programme.title: Made in Britain

programme.title: On Her Majesty's Secret Service (1969)
programme.desc: While on leave, James Bond prevents a young woman - Teresa 'Tracy' Draco - from committing suicide. Her father is Marc-Ange Draco, the head of a powerful crime syndicate who is impressed by Bond and wants him to protect his daughter by marrying her. In exchange, he offers Bond information that will lead him to master criminal and arch-enemy Ernst Stavro Blofeld, who is intent on destroying world peace by means of a lethal virus unless he receives an international amnesty for his crimes. Spy adventure, starring George Lazenby in his only outing as 007, alongside Telly Savalas, Diana Rigg, Gabrielle Ferzetti and Bernard Lee

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Couples compete in Bognor Regis, West Sussex, with Carl and Brianha kicking off proceedings with an evening inspired by the glitz and glamour of the French Riviera. programme.title: I Am
programme.desc: A single woman with a successful career (Humans' Gemma Chan) struggles with her mother's demands that she start a family before it is too late, and feels under further pressure as she watches her friends settle down and have children. Whilst in their company, Ben drives cattle on horseback, digs out a dam to ensure the family's hydro-electricity supply and gets to grips with the annual deer cull, and hears how Iona's past prepared her for this extraordinary life in the wilderness

programme.title: Pompeii: The New Revelations
programme.desc: Archaeologist Raksha Dave and historian Dan Snow uncover the untold story of the aftermath of the famous eruption in AD79, finding out what happened when the ash and dust settled. Dan squeezes into a network of illegally dug tunnels that burrow into a huge unexcavated villa, while Raksha is given privileged access to the highly restricted Regio V area of the town

programme.title: Ancient Mysteries
programme.desc: A look at what caused the downfall of the Ancient Egyptians in 30BC, with new evidence revealing a cataclysmic event that shook the world and sowed the seeds of the empire's demise. Egyptologist Joe Manning and climate historian Francis Ludlow have traced this misfortune to an event in 44BC, when a colossal volcano erupted somewhere in the Tropics

The programme also includes film of a getaway in which robbers tried to evade the law by driving at 180mph

programme.title: Britain's Great Cathedrals with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: The actor visits York Minster, which over the past 800 years, has been a backdrop to some of the most dramatic events in British history. It houses more than half of all the world's medieval glass, including the Rose Window, commissioned by Henry VIII, and the East Window, the largest stained glass window in the world. A succession of outspoken bishops of York have defied Nazis, married kings and queens, and more recently championed equality with Archbishop John Sentamu, the country's first black bishop, consecrating the world's first female bishop, Libby Lane, in 2014.

programme.desc: Alex Polizzi visits the Sea Croft, a struggling B&B in Lytham St Anne's, Lancashire. Two years ago, owner Georgina gave up a career teaching business studies with a dream of running a successful guest house. Now, a £50,000 bill for unforeseen repairs and dismal occupancy has left her struggling to run the business. With her immaculate rooms lying empty, self-confessed neat freak Georgina cannot understand where she is going wrong. Alex can see the potential for success but getting Georgina to stop focusing on little details and start seeing the bigger picture does not prove easy

programme.title: Britain's Great Cathedrals with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: Built more than 1,400 years ago, Canterbury Cathedral is both a holy place and a World Heritage site. It is the 'mother church' for more than 85 million people, and one of the biggest tourist attractions in the UK with nearly a million visitors a year. Tony tells the tale of the cathedral's founding and how, after the cathedral burnt down, it was rebuilt by the Normans as a symbol of their power. He also explores how it became wealthy thanks to its role as a major site of pilgrimage following the infamous murder of Archbishop Thomas Becket

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Property expert Ann Maurice tackles a three-bedroom terraced Victorian house in Norwich which, thanks to a vast array of deeply personal belongings, overpowering paintwork and too much furniture, has been languishing on the market for three months.
